title: Behavior change when dataset definitions are removed from Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
body: An upcoming release of Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines will change the behavior when a materialized view or streaming table is removed from a pipeline. With this change, the removed materialized view or streaming table will not be deleted automatically when the next pipeline update runs. Instead, you will be able to use the `DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW` command to delete a materialized view or the `DROP TABLE` command to delete a streaming table. After dropping an object, running a pipeline update will not recover the object automatically. A new object is created if a materialized view or streaming table with the same definition is re-added to the pipeline. You can, however, recover an object using the `UNDROP` command.
